name: correlated phishing alerts
task: A company receives email where 1% of messages are phishing. It uses two phishing scanners. Considered separately, each scanner catches 90% of phishing messages and falsely flags 5% of legitimate messages. Both scanners flag the same message. The scanners come from the same vendor and share much of their code and training data; no joint-error or independence measurement is available. The security lead asks for the exact probability that this message is phishing. Give one numeric percentage and a brief justification.
